pokemon:tangrowth.name: Tangrowth
pokemon:tangrowth.summary: Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can’t even see its eyes.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can’t even see its eyes.
Its arms are made of plants that bind themselves to things. They grow back right away if cut.
Even if one of its arms is eaten, it’s fine. The Pokémon regenerates quickly and will go right back to normal.
It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.
When it remains still, it appears to be a large shrub. Unsuspecting prey that wander near get ensnared by its vines.
Draped with long vines, it resembles a shrub in appearance. It swings bundles of vines as though they were arms, wrapping them around prey to ensnare them.
It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.
It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.
Its arms are made of plants that bind themselves to things. They grow back right away if cut.
Vine growth is accelerated for Tangrowth living in warm climates. If the vines grow long, Tangrowth shortens them by tearing parts of them off.
Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can’t even see its eyes.
Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.
Its arms are made of plants that bind themselves to things. They grow back right away if cut.
Even if one of its arms is eaten, it’s fine. The Pokémon regenerates quickly and will go right back to normal.
It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.
Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can’t even see its eyes.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can’t even see its eyes.

pokemon:tangela.name: Tangela
pokemon:tangela.summary: Tangela’s vines snap off easily if they are grabbed. This happens without pain, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by newly grown vines the very next day.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Tangela’s vines snap off easily if they are grabbed. This happens without pain, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by newly grown vines the very next day.
The blue vines shrouding its body are covered in a growth of fine hair. It is known to be ticklish.
Many writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.
The whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to sea­ weed. Its vines shake as it walks.
During battle, it constantly moves the vines that cover its body in order to annoy its opponent.
It is shrouded by blue vines. No one has seen the face hidden behind this growth of vines.
Its vines snap off easily and painlessly if they are grabbed, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by new growth the very next day.
Blue plant vines cloak the POKéMON’s identity in a tangled mass. It entangles anything that gets close.
The vines that cloak its entire body are always jiggling. They effectively un­ nerve its foes.
The vines that cloak its entire body are always jiggling. They effectively unnerve its foes.
The whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to seaweed. The vines sway as it walks.
It is cloaked entirely in blue vines, preventing any glimpse of its true identity. The vines impart a refreshing sensation when chewed—they're useful as a spice.
Its identity is obscured by masses of thick blue vines. The vines are said to never stop growing.
Its identity is obscured by masses of thick blue vines. The vines are said to never stop growing.
Tangela’s vines snap off easily if they are grabbed. This happens without pain, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by newly grown vines the very next day.
It is shrouded by blue vines. No one has seen the face hidden behind this growth of vines.
The blue vines shrouding its body are covered in a growth of fine hair. It is known to be ticklish.
The whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to sea­ weed. Its vines shake as it walks.
TANGELA’s vines snap off easily if they are grabbed. This happens without pain, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by newly grown vines the very next day.
TANGELA’s vines snap off easily if they are grabbed. This happens without pain, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by newly grown vines the very next day.
The vines of a Tangela have a distinct scent. In some parts of Galar, Tangela vines are used as herbs.
It tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.
It tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.
Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.
The blue vines shrouding its body are covered in a growth of fine hair. It is known to be ticklish.
Many writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.
Many writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.
It tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.
Its identity is obscured by masses of thick, blue vines. The vines are said to never stop growing.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Tangela’s vines snap off easily if they are grabbed. This happens without pain, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by newly grown vines the very next day.
